strengths: Clear identification of three specific real-world objects with authentic contexts
Explicit links between each object and the chosen IA prompt about knowledge dissemination challenges
Concrete illustrative details (e.g. DNA metaphor, tangyuan homophone, Rafah slogan) showing TOK ideas applied to tangible situations
Justifications for inclusion are generally supported by relevant explanation and reference back to the IA prompt
Coherent overarching thread demonstrating how TOK manifests in real-world examples
weaknesses: Explanations sometimes lack precision and clarity, with assertions unsupported by external evidence
Uneven use of TOK terminology and conceptual frameworks limits analytical rigor
Citation practice is inconsistent (missing dates, incomplete references) weakening credibility
Analysis across objects remains episodic with limited synthesis in conclusion
Some proposed solutions are stated without evaluation of feasibility or pedagogical grounding
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The official examinerâs total mark of 6 corresponds to the 5â6 band descriptor: the submission clearly identifies three objects in context and makes well-supported links to the IA prompt, but falls short of the precision, consistent evidence, and conceptual depth required for higher bands. Strengths include concrete examples and coherent connections, while weaknessesâuneven citations, limited TOK vocabulary, and episodic analysisâjustify placing the work at the upper end of the 5â6 band. Allocating the full 6 marks to this single gradable criterion both reflects the examinerâs final mark and aligns with the strand-level feedback.

annotations.comment: The argument that news reporting âclarifies circumstancesâ would benefit from a concrete example or data showing when and how reportage corrected public misunderstanding.
The progression from simple analogies to technical terms (e.g., ATP) is described, but the student fails to situate this within any recognized educational or pedagogical theory.
The video thumbnail is described in detail, but there is no citation of the upload date or a link to validate the example, weakening the credibility of the evidence.
Linking the homophone âtangyuanâ to âtuan yuanâ demonstrates an insightful awareness of linguistic barriers and how language affects cultural knowledge dissemination.
The commentary relies on personal experience to justify inclusion but does not address potential bias or consider how outsiders might verify the cultural significance independently.
The student correctly identifies a trade-off between simplicity and accuracy, showing understanding of how knowledge reliability can be compromised when tailored to different audience competencies.

The proposed solution of âappropriate delivery of educationâ is stated but remains vague; the student should illustrate specific pedagogical strategies to minimize the simplicityâaccuracy trade-off.
The conclusion restates earlier solutions without synthesizing insights across all three objects or explicitly revisiting the IA promptâs wording to demonstrate cohesive understanding.
Object 1 is clearly identified with its full title, showing the specific source and framing it in the real-world context of WIREDâs â5 Levelsâ series.
The student restates the prompt as the title but does not offer a clear thesis or roadmap indicating how the three objects will collectively address the challenges of disseminating knowledge.
The analysis acknowledges challenges of communicating science but lacks explicit TOK terminology (e.g., ways of knowing, knowledge frameworks) to deepen the conceptual linkage to the IA prompt.
The example of âDNAâ as an âinstruction manualâ illustrates oversimplification yet the student does not explore potential misconceptions arising from this metaphor, limiting analytical depth.
The reference format is inconsistent (missing publisher details for the HT article and no access date); adherence to a recognized citation style would strengthen reliability.
An incomplete sentence at the end of the paragraph indicates lapses in editing and disrupts the coherence of the argument.
The conclusion of this object reiterates earlier points without reflecting on broader implications or connecting back to distinct TOK concepts, reducing analytical rigour.
Object 2 is precisely named and situated in its authentic context, showing a clear real-world scenario (a Google search page) relevant to the IA prompt.
The screenshot description is exhaustive but remains descriptive. The student should analyse how the layout of search results influences the reliability or bias of accessed knowledge.
The student offers a practical solutionâcelebrating Lantern Festival in schoolsâto overcome cultural knowledge gaps, showing reflexivity in applying the IA prompt.
The suggestion lacks evaluation of feasibility or scalability; the student should discuss potential challenges of implementing school-wide cultural celebrations.
Object 3 is well introduced with full bibliographic details, situating the article in its geopolitical and media context, which strengthens its relevance.
The description of the news website interface is thorough but overly descriptive; analysis should focus on how media framing choices influence audience perception of the Rafah crisis.

While personal experience enriches the narrative, the student should critically assess potential confirmation bias and consider how non-Chinese knowers might independently verify the cultural significance.

The link drawn between social-media virality of the slogan âAll Eyes on Rafahâ and the oversimplification of complex conflict reflects a clear connection to the prompt about accuracy in knowledge dissemination.
Only one full reference is provided for Object 3; the student should include citations for the WIRED video and any web pages used for the Tangyuan search to ensure academic rigour.
The closing statement on AI tools is a useful academic integrity note but could be relocated to a declaration section rather than mixed with references.
The visual analysis does not reference the videoâs publication date or channel metrics (views, likes), missing an opportunity to discuss its reach and impact on knowledge dissemination.

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content: The exhibition does not reach the standard described by the other levels or does not use one of the IA prompts provided.

The exhibition presents
three objects, but the
real-world contexts of
these objects are not
stated, or the images
presented may be
highly generic images of
types of object rather
than of specific
real-world objects. Links
between the objects
and the selected IA
prompt are made, but
these are minimal,
tenuous, or it is not
clear what the student is
trying to convey.
There is very little
justification offered for
the inclusion of each
object in the exhibition.
The commentary on the
objects is highly
descriptive or consists
only of unsupported
assertions.

The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.
